author: Wanja Zemke
description: Not anOther Pdf Exporter. Export notes to publication-ready PDFs via Pandoc and the Eisvogel LaTeX template. Docker-powered toolchain, no LaTeX install required. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: Wanja Zemke
manifest.description: Not anOther Pdf Exporter. Export notes to publication-ready PDFs via Pandoc and the Eisvogel LaTeX template. Custom branding, glossaries, transclusions, and cross-references — Docker-powered toolchain, no LaTeX install required.
manifest.name: Nope

name: Nope

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- **Citation notes:** add `citekey` to a note and link it with `[[Note]]` — BibTeX is generated automatically, works alongside any `.bib`.
- **Unicode-safe builds:** Greek letters, math symbols and sub/superscripts no longer crash the export.
- **Custom templates:** `"[[my-template]]"` resolves without the `.tex` extension; `article`-based templates survive unnumbered headings.
- **Preview** remembers Auto-render & Follow-cursor across close/reopen.
- **Bases** render more reliably
- **Timer**  each export logs phase timings to `build/timer.csv`.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: Fix community Review Error - Using fileManager.trashFile(tempFile)

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### New: Bases

Embed an Obsidian base view into a document with `![[Base.base#View]]`. What it produces depends on the `latex-env` of the surrounding note:

- No `latex-env` — every note the view returns is transcluded, in the view's order (use a base as a document spine / table of contents).
- `latex-env: table` + `caption` — the view is rendered as a numbered table with exactly the columns, order and sort of that view.

The query (filters, formulas, sort, columns) is evaluated by Obsidian's real Bases engine at export time, so what the view shows is what you get.

### Breaking

- Minimum Obsidian version is now **1.10.0** — the Bases API requires it, and the core Bases plugin must be enabled.

### Notes

- During export, a referenced base view briefly opens in a background tab while Obsidian renders it, then closes again. This short flash is expected. A base only yields data once Obsidian actually renders it.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: - Fit-tables (longtable: false) no longer fail with Unknown float option 'H' in custom templates — float is now part of the NOPE-IMPORTS block.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## Bugs
- Jump-to-cursor works again in embedded notes (previously main file only).
- Text selection in the PDF preview fixed cleanly.

## Maintenance
- Community plugin check (no dynamic `<script>`, no `!important`).

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
### Features
- **Custom LaTeX templates**: point a doc/branding note at your own `.tex` via `nope-template`; `Create custom LaTeX template` drops a ready `nope_minimal.tex` starter. Without it, Eisvogel as before.
- **Table layout**: new `longtable` key: default scales wide tables to fit one page (no more column overflow); `true` for long, multi-page tables.
- **Quick-insert commands**: `Add table / theorem / lemma / definition / proof / mermaid diagram / equation / glossary term / abbreviation` scaffold an atomic note with the right frontmatter and drop the embed at your cursor.
- **PDF preview on pdf.js**: renders reliably (incl. Windows), selectable text, working links, plus a toolbar with zoom, page number, a render-options menu and a download button.
- **Self-updating Docker image**: rebuilds automatically when an update changes the pipeline; no manual rebuild.

### Fixes
- Cross-references resolve by type ("Lemma 1.1", "Definition 1.3" instead of "Theorem"); proof references now render as links.
- `toc` / `toc-own-page` are respected again, and the stray blank page at the end (no glossary) is gone.
- Emoji and other unsupported characters no longer crash the export — they're dropped with a notice.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
- Custom LaTeX templates: set `nope-template: "[[…]]"` in the document or branding frontmatter to use your own `.tex` template; without it, Eisvogel is used. Command `Create custom LaTeX template` writes a `nope_minimal.tex` starter to the vault.
- Tables: new `longtable` frontmatter key. Default (`false`) scales wide tables to fit the page width; `true` uses a multi-page table with a repeating header.
- New commands to insert atomic notes: `Add table / theorem / lemma / definition / proof / mermaid diagram / equation / glossary term / abbreviation`. Each creates a note with the required frontmatter and inserts the embed/reference at the cursor.
- Cross-references now resolve by type ("Lemma 1.1", "Definition 1.3" instead of "Theorem"); references to proofs render as links.
- Branding template: more documented frontmatter keys (Eisvogel and Pandoc); key descriptions moved from the frontmatter into the note body.

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description:
## New: Live PDF preview

This release adds an Overleaf-style PDF preview directly inside Obsidian.

### Features

- **PDF preview view** — new command *Open PDF preview* opens the rendered
  PDF of the current note in a split pane. Render with one click; download
  and print via the built-in PDF toolbar.
- **Auto-render** — toggle it on and the preview re-renders automatically
  whenever the note or any of its embedded notes, images, glossary terms,
  or diagrams change. Dependency tracking comes straight from the pipeline,
  so it always matches what actually ends up in the PDF.
- **Follow cursor** — the PDF scrolls to the section your cursor is in,
  automatically (toggle) or on demand via the command
  *Sync PDF preview to cursor* (hotkey-friendly). Jumps are anchor-precise,
  not just page-precise, and the position survives re-renders.
